system: single series RLC resonator with declared LC storage coordinates

ideal: charge q and flux_phi as storage coordinates
practical: ring-down linewidth or Q-factor as damping metadata

control_variables.name: R
control_variables.meaning: series resistance recorded as damping, not storage curvature

regime_assumptions: capacitor and inductor storage are ideal quadratic terms
resistance is linear damping and is not inserted into the static Hessian
drive, radiation, and nonlinear component behaviour are outside this card
state coordinates are declared storage coordinates
equation_hook: U(q, flux_phi) = q^2/(2 Ccap) + flux_phi^2/(2 L); damping metadata R is outside U

units_and_dimensions.R: ohm = V A^-1, not a Hessian unit

coordinate_note: Charge and flux have different units; resistance is a dissipative response parameter and must not be compared with Hessian entries.

module_object.description: diagonal LC storage Hessian with R recorded outside the static module object

derivation_status: exact for the declared LC storage energy; damping is deliberately excluded from H

ceiling_or_reference.description: no hidden coordinate is declared in this card

positive_scope: This card exposes the storage part of a damped resonator as the same exact quadratic LC object while keeping damping out of the Hessian.
boundary: It does not describe dissipative linewidth, driven response, transient fitting, or energy loss as a static precision object.
failure_mode: The attachment fails if R or drive response is treated as static storage curvature rather than damping metadata.
next_measurement: Measure Ccap, L, and R separately; use Ccap and L for storage geometry and reserve R for a later dynamic response layer.
